headline: This Month’s Top Stocks Have One Thing in Common
summary: The way to stock market gains so far in March has been to bet on the biggest natural-gas users.  U.S. natural gas prices have remained relatively stable compared with those overseas, giving big American gas consumers an advantage over global competitors since war began in the Middle East.  CF Industries, which makes nitrogen fertilizer, is the S&P 500's top performing stock so far in March, up more than 30%.

headline: Top S&P500 movers in Friday's pre-market session
summary: As the US market prepares to open on Friday, let's get an early glimpse into the pre-market session and identify the S&P500 stocks leading the pack in terms of gains and losses.

headline: US Stock Market Today S&P 500 Futures Slip As Rate And Oil Jitters Grow
summary: The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Friday, Mar, 13 2026 US stock futures are pointing lower this morning, with contracts tied to the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 in the red as investors react to a fresh jump in government bond yields and higher oil prices. The US 10 year yield is around 4.26%, which means borrowing costs for things like credit cards and car loans remain relatively high. Futures linked to market volatility and European bank stocks also signal nerves about the global...

headline: Iran War Ripples Across Global Commodities, BMO Warns
summary: BMO warns Middle East conflict is the biggest oil market event in decades. See how supply risks are shifting global fundamentals.

headline: LyondellBasel (LYB) Climbs 10.3% as Citi Says ‘Buy’, Hikes Price Target by 55%
summary: LyondellBasell Industries NV (NYSE:LYB) is one of the 10 Stock Winners in a Brutal Market. LyondellBasell soared by 10.33 percent on Thursday to close at $74.33 apiece, as investors took heart from Citigroup’s bullish stance for its stock, having upgraded its rating and price target by 55 percent.
